informal: Show that the collection $\{(a,b) \mid a < b, a \text{ and } b \text{ rational}\}$ is a basis that generates a topology different from the lower limit topology on $\mathbb{R}$.
lean:
theorem Munkres_exercise_13_8b (T : Set (Set ℝ))
  (hT : T = {S : Set ℝ | ∃ a b : ℚ, a < b ∧ S = Ioo ↑a ↑b}) :
  IsTopologicalBasis T ∧
  generateFrom T ≠
  lower_limit_topology ℝ
reasoning: The informal statement (as given) refers to the collection of open intervals (a,b) with rational endpoints a<b. The Lean signature defines T as exactly {Ioo ↑a ↑b | a b : ℚ, a < b}, where Ioo is the open interval — matching the informal "(a,b) with a and b rational". The conclusion has two parts: (i) T is a topological basis (IsTopologicalBasis), and (ii) the topology generated by T is not equal to the lower-limit topology on ℝ — which is exactly what the informal statement asks to show. Quantifier structure, types (ℚ for endpoints, ℝ for the ambient space), and the inequality constraint are all preserved. No extra hypotheses are introduced and none are dropped.
